ingredients.item:
Green fresh spring rolls (cuốn diếp) are light and incredibly refreshing on hot days. I first tried this dish at a vegan restaurant in Ho Chi Minh City and was amazed at how tasty it was. Mustard greens (Brassica jucea) refer to a variety of plants that differ sharply in heat, flavor, and appearance. This recipe calls for cải cay (Vietnamese) or juk gai choy (Cantonese), which is a leafy, light green romaine with a narrow, celery-shaped stem. It supplies a remarkable peppery taste with just a hint of mustard.
SKILL LEVELModerate|YIELD4 servings

SPRING ROLLS
7 ounces (200 g) firm tofu
¼ cup (60 ml) plus 1 tablespoon vegetable oil, divided

1 tablespoon minced scallion (white part only), plus 5 scallions (green parts only)
3½ ounces (100 g) peeled and shredded carrot, divided
ingredients.original:
Green fresh spring rolls (cuốn diếp) are light and incredibly refreshing on hot days. I first tried this dish at a vegan restaurant in Ho Chi Minh City and was amazed at how tasty it was. Mustard greens (Brassica jucea) refer to a variety of plants that differ sharply in heat, flavor, and appearance. This recipe calls for cải cay (Vietnamese) or juk gai choy (Cantonese), which is a leafy, light green romaine with a narrow, celery-shaped stem. It supplies a remarkable peppery taste with just a hint of mustard.
SKILL LEVELModerate|YIELD4 servings

SPRING ROLLS
7 ounces (200 g) firm tofu
¼ cup (60 ml) plus 1 tablespoon vegetable oil, divided

1 tablespoon minced scallion (white part only), plus 5 scallions (green parts only)
3½ ounces (100 g) peeled and shredded carrot, divided

ingredients.item: 1½ ounces (45 g) dried wood ear, oyster, or shiitake mushrooms, cut into ¼-inch (6 mm) strips
ingredients.original: 1½ ounces (45 g) dried wood ear, oyster, or shiitake mushrooms, cut into ¼-inch (6 mm) strips

ingredients.item: 1½ teaspoons sugar, divided
ingredients.original: 1½ teaspoons sugar, divided

ingredients.item: 1 teaspoon vegetable or mushroom stock powder
ingredients.original: 1 teaspoon vegetable or mushroom stock powder

ingredients.item: 1 tablespoon distilled white vinegar
ingredients.original: 1 tablespoon distilled white vinegar

ingredients.item: 7 ounces (200 g) dried rice vermicelli noodles
ingredients.original: 7 ounces (200 g) dried rice vermicelli noodles

ingredients.item: 2 sweet potatoes, boiled until tender and cut into sticks about the size of french fries
ingredients.original: 2 sweet potatoes, boiled until tender and cut into sticks about the size of french fries

ingredients.item: 3½ ounces (100 g) fresh herbs (such as mint, Asian basil, and/or perilla leaves)
ingredients.original: 3½ ounces (100 g) fresh herbs (such as mint, Asian basil, and/or perilla leaves)

ingredients.item: 1 pound (454 g) fresh mustard greens (cải cay or juk gai choy; see Note), thick part of stems removed and rinsed
ingredients.original: 1 pound (454 g) fresh mustard greens (cải cay or juk gai choy; see Note), thick part of stems removed and rinsed

ingredients.item: ¼ cup (120 ml) fermented soybean sauce
ingredients.original: ¼ cup (120 ml) fermented soybean sauce

ingredients.item: ¼ cup (50 g) sugar
ingredients.original: ¼ cup (50 g) sugar

ingredients.item: 1 tablespoon peanut butter
ingredients.original: 1 tablespoon peanut butter

ingredients.item: ½ cup (120 ml) water
ingredients.original: ½ cup (120 ml) water

ingredients.item: ¼ cup (32 g) toasted sesame seeds
ingredients.original: ¼ cup (32 g) toasted sesame seeds

ingredients.item: 1 bird’s eye chili pepper, minced (optional)
ingredients.original: 1 bird’s eye chili pepper, minced (optional)

ingredients.item: 1. MAKE THE SPRING ROLLS Cut the tofu into ½-inch (1 cm) thick “steaks.”
ingredients.original: 1. MAKE THE SPRING ROLLS Cut the tofu into ½-inch (1 cm) thick “steaks.”

ingredients.item: 2. In a medium skillet, heat ¼ cup (60 ml) of the vegetable oil over medium heat. When hot, cook the tofu in batches until golden brown on both sides (5 to 8 minutes per side). When done, transfer tofu to a paper towel–lined plate to drain. Let cool completely, and then cut the steaks into ½-inch (1 cm) wide sticks.
ingredients.original: 2. In a medium skillet, heat ¼ cup (60 ml) of the vegetable oil over medium heat. When hot, cook the tofu in batches until golden brown on both sides (5 to 8 minutes per side). When done, transfer tofu to a paper towel–lined plate to drain. Let cool completely, and then cut the steaks into ½-inch (1 cm) wide sticks.

ingredients.item: 3. Discard remaining oil in skillet, add remaining 1 tablespoon vegetable oil and heat over low heat. Add 1 tablespoon of minced scallion (white part) and cook and stir until golden brown (about 1 minute).
ingredients.original: 3. Discard remaining oil in skillet, add remaining 1 tablespoon vegetable oil and heat over low heat. Add 1 tablespoon of minced scallion (white part) and cook and stir until golden brown (about 1 minute).

ingredients.item: 4. Add half the carrot and cook and stir until softened (1 to 2 minutes). Add mushrooms, ½ teaspoon of the sugar, stock powder, and salt, and stir well. Add the prepared tofu, gently toss to combine, and then transfer to a plate to cool.
ingredients.original: 4. Add half the carrot and cook and stir until softened (1 to 2 minutes). Add mushrooms, ½ teaspoon of the sugar, stock powder, and salt, and stir well. Add the prepared tofu, gently toss to combine, and then transfer to a plate to cool.

ingredients.item: 5. In a medium bowl, combine remaining carrot with remaining 1 teaspoon sugar and vinegar. Mix well and set aside for 10 minutes.
ingredients.original: 5. In a medium bowl, combine remaining carrot with remaining 1 teaspoon sugar and vinegar. Mix well and set aside for 10 minutes.

ingredients.item: 6. Fill a medium saucepan halfway with water and bring to a rolling boil over high heat. Prepare a bowl of ice water and set nearby. Drop scallions (green part) into boiling water for 30 seconds, or until wilted. Use a wire spider or slotted spoon to transfer to bowl of ice water. Drain and set aside.
ingredients.original: 6. Fill a medium saucepan halfway with water and bring to a rolling boil over high heat. Prepare a bowl of ice water and set nearby. Drop scallions (green part) into boiling water for 30 seconds, or until wilted. Use a wire spider or slotted spoon to transfer to bowl of ice water. Drain and set aside.

ingredients.item: 7. Cook the dried vermicelli noodles following package instructions.
ingredients.original: 7. Cook the dried vermicelli noodles following package instructions.

ingredients.item: 8. Place tofu-mushroom mixture, sweet potatoes, fresh herbs, noodles, mustard greens, and green scallions on separate plates.
ingredients.original: 8. Place tofu-mushroom mixture, sweet potatoes, fresh herbs, noodles, mustard greens, and green scallions on separate plates.

ingredients.item: 9. Place one mustard green leaf, dark side down, on a flat work surface or cutting board. Place a bit of each filling ingredient—rice vermicelli, 1 tablespoon of tofu-mushroom mixture, 1 sweet potato stick, pickled carrot, and fresh herbs—on one end of the leaf. Lift up the end with the fillings over the fillings, and then gently roll it up.
ingredients.original: 9. Place one mustard green leaf, dark side down, on a flat work surface or cutting board. Place a bit of each filling ingredient—rice vermicelli, 1 tablespoon of tofu-mushroom mixture, 1 sweet potato stick, pickled carrot, and fresh herbs—on one end of the leaf. Lift up the end with the fillings over the fillings, and then gently roll it up.

ingredients.item: 10. Use a leaf layer of blanched scallion as a string to tie up the roll, and then repeat with the remaining mustard leaves, fillings, and scallions. Lay the rolls parallel to each other and trim off the top and bottom edges to make them uniform in size.
ingredients.original: 10. Use a leaf layer of blanched scallion as a string to tie up the roll, and then repeat with the remaining mustard leaves, fillings, and scallions. Lay the rolls parallel to each other and trim off the top and bottom edges to make them uniform in size.

ingredients.item: 11. MAKE THE DIPPING SAUCE In a small saucepan, heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil over low heat. When hot, add 1 tablespoon of minced scallion and cook and stir until fragrant (about 30 seconds).
ingredients.original: 11. MAKE THE DIPPING SAUCE In a small saucepan, heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil over low heat. When hot, add 1 tablespoon of minced scallion and cook and stir until fragrant (about 30 seconds).

ingredients.item: 12. Add fermented soybean sauce, ¼ cup (50 g) of sugar, peanut butter, and water, and stir well. Simmer until smooth and thickened (1 to 2 minutes).
ingredients.original: 12. Add fermented soybean sauce, ¼ cup (50 g) of sugar, peanut butter, and water, and stir well. Simmer until smooth and thickened (1 to 2 minutes).

ingredients.item: 13. Transfer to a dipping sauce bowl, stir in sesame seeds, top with minced chili pepper (if using) and serve with green spring rolls.
ingredients.original: 13. Transfer to a dipping sauce bowl, stir in sesame seeds, top with minced chili pepper (if using) and serve with green spring rolls.
